title: Scope and Application
text: The California Energy Code applies to the new construction, alteration, addition, and repair of every building or structure or any appurtenances connected or attached to such buildings or structures throughout the state. Compliance demonstrated via the prescriptive, performance, or additions/alterations methods.

title: Mandatory Insulation - Residential
text: Insulation R-values for low-rise residential buildings by climate zone: ceilings R-30 to R-38 minimum; wood-frame walls R-13 to R-21 cavity plus continuous insulation per CZ; raised floors R-19 to R-30; below-grade walls R-13 cavity. Continuous insulation requirements increase in colder zones (CZ 16) and where steel framing is used.

title: Photovoltaic Requirement for New Residential
text: Newly constructed low-rise residential buildings shall have a photovoltaic system that meets the minimum qualifying PV system size in kW DC defined by the equation in Section 150.1(c)14, based on conditioned floor area and climate zone. Exceptions for shaded roof area, deed-restricted affordable housing, and certain alterations.

title: Residential Lighting Requirements
text: All installed luminaires in low-rise residential buildings shall be high-efficacy in accordance with the criteria of Reference Joint Appendix JA8. Required dimmer or vacancy sensor controls in rooms with installed lighting; exterior lighting must be controlled by motion sensor and photocontrol or astronomical time-clock.

title: Nonresidential Envelope - Prescriptive U-factor
text: Maximum U-factors and minimum insulation R-values for nonresidential buildings by climate zone per Table 140.3-B. Roofs/ceilings U=0.039 (R-30+) climate zones 1-15. Walls U=0.071 to U=0.082 depending on mass and framing. Fenestration maximum U-factor 0.36 to 0.46 with SHGC 0.22 to 0.45 by orientation and climate zone.

title: Lighting Power Density - Nonresidential
text: Interior installed lighting power for the entire building shall not exceed the allowable lighting power calculated by either the Area Category Method or the Tailored Method, with allowances per Table 140.6-C. Example LPDs: office enclosed 0.7 W/sf; office open 0.7; classroom 0.9; corridor 0.5; warehouse 0.6; retail sales 1.05.

title: EV Charging Infrastructure - Residential
text: Newly constructed one- and two-family dwellings with attached private garages shall be provided with at least one Level 2 EV-ready space. EV-ready means a listed raceway capable of accommodating a 208/240V branch circuit terminating at a junction box, receptacle, or EV charger. Service panel and subpanel shall reserve capacity for the EV branch circuit (minimum 40A 208/240V dedicated branch circuit) with overcurrent protection and conductor sized per NEC.

title: Water Conserving Plumbing Fixtures - Residential
text: Residential plumbing fixtures and fixture fittings shall comply with the following maximum flow rates: water closets 1.28 gallons per flush (gpf); urinals 0.125 gpf; showerheads 1.8 gallons per minute (gpm) at 80 psi; lavatory faucets 1.2 gpm at 60 psi (1.5 gpm allowed for nonresidential); kitchen faucets 1.8 gpm at 60 psi.

title: EV Charging - Nonresidential
text: New construction shall facilitate future installation of electric vehicle supply equipment per Table 5.106.5.3.3. Number of EV-capable and EV-ready spaces scales with total parking. Example: 26-50 spaces = 4 EV-capable, 2 EV-ready, 1 EVSE installed; 51-75 = 6, 3, 1; 101-150 = 12, 6, 2.

title: Wildland-Urban Interface Construction
text: New buildings located in a Wildland-Urban Interface Fire Area or designated Fire Hazard Severity Zone (Moderate, High, Very High) shall be constructed with ignition-resistant exterior wall coverings, Class A roof coverings, ember-resistant or noncombustible vents, dual-paned tempered or fire-resistive glazing, and noncombustible eave protection. The first 5 feet of any attached deck or 6-foot exterior wall measured from the ground shall be noncombustible.

title: Defensible Space
text: A person who owns, leases, controls, operates, or maintains a building or structure in, upon, or adjoining mountainous, forest-covered, brush-covered, grass-covered land, or any land that is covered with flammable material, shall maintain defensible space of 100 feet from each side of the structure. Zone 0 (0-5 ft): ember-resistant noncombustible. Zone 1 (5-30 ft): lean, clean, green - remove dead/dying vegetation. Zone 2 (30-100 ft): reduced fuels - mowed/limbed/spaced.
